title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite social media discourse declaring it so, pointing to rising Google search volume and real improvements from semantic search in Cursor. The core issue was a narrow definition of retrieval, which should include vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively by agents. The key tradeoff is whether to cache embeddings based on query volume, as exemplified by Cursor's upfront indexing versus Claude Code's per-session grep approach.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer pushes back on the claim that RAG is dead, citing rising Google search volume and measurable gains from semantic search in Cursor. The argument hinges on a broader definition of retrieval: not just vector search, but full text search, glob, regex, and filters applied iteratively by agents. The central tradeoff is whether to cache embeddings, which depends on query volume, contrasting Cursor's upfront indexing with Claude Code's per-session grep.
